topThreeIssues: Math direction error: narration says 'expected input is p times larger' at test time -- correct direction is 1/p times larger (training activations are p times smaller)
Canvas clutter: net_full from t=0 is never erased; test-time section overlays new equations on a busy canvas
Ensemble tree grandchildren described as 'smaller unit subsets' without naming the actual subsets -- specify h1/h3/h2/h4 explicitly
Beat at t=175 overloaded: introduces orthogonality requirement, appeals to C symmetry, closes ranking logic in one beat -- split into two
E[xx^T]=C asserted without justification; for stated audience this step needs an explicit narrated note about centered data and (1/n)X^TX
n in covariance definition never named as sample size -- one-clause aside fixes it
Payoff statistic is factually wrong: naive fib(50) makes ~2x10^10 calls (~20 billion), not '>2 trillion' -- propagates into payoff, narration, and board write at t=35
memo_table and tabulation_sweep both assigned region bottom-center, creating layout collision -- shift one to bottom-right
fib5_tree erased at t=260 with 10s remaining -- primary visual anchor removed during closing summary

Probability tree drawn at 5s with all numeric values populated before narration introduces those numbers at 145s -- creates a 2-minute forward reference; either defer numeric labels or use placeholders early
Payoff says 'less than 9%' but actual posterior is 9.016% -- slightly above 9% -- correct framing or change worked example
Beat at 175s packs normalizer computation, posterior result, and rare-prior insight into one beat -- split into two for the surprise conclusion to land
Beat at t=70 overloaded: tightness condition + E-step + responsibilities + Q-function in one 50s beat -- split tightness/E-step from Q-function

Two likelihood-surface diagrams (likelihood_lowerbound_curve and em_iteration_diagram) are separate static snapshots -- merge into one evolving canvas to strengthen the iterative-ascent visual narrative
Convergence beat (t=210) and summary beat (t=248) restate the sandwich inequality and local-max conclusion redundantly -- differentiate by foregrounding initialization sensitivity in summary
